basic_details.job_title: TIFR Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 35 Posts, Last Date 4 July 2026
basic_details.short_information: Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR), Mumbai has released inviting online applications from eligible Indian nationals for 35 posts at its Colaba, Mumbai campus. Posts on offer span scientific, technical, administrative, and support categories including Scientific Officer (C), Junior Engineer (B), Scientific Assistant (B), Administrative Assistant (B), Tradesman (B), Clerk (A), Work Assistant, Security Guard, and more. The last date to apply online is 4 July 2026 at tifr.res.in. Pay scales range from ₹36,360 to ₹1,18,110 as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix.

age_limit.details: Each post under TIFR Recruitment 2026 carries specific eligibility requirements. The age limit is reckoned as on the closing date for receipt of applications (4 July 2026). Knowledge of GFR and CPWD procurement procedures desirable. Age: Maximum 33 years.

qualification.details: Each post under TIFR Recruitment 2026 carries specific eligibility requirements. The age limit is reckoned as on the closing date for receipt of applications (4 July 2026) . Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India rules for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen/Government servant candidates.

salary_details.details: Candidates wondering about appointment duration can note that for posts at Sr. Nos. 1 to 7 and 12 to 17, the initial appointment will be for 3 years including a 1-year probation period. Continuation beyond 3 years is subject to comprehensive performance review; upon successful review, the appointment continues till superannuation at age 60. Post No. 30 (Project Administrative Assistant) is a temporary, non-pensionable contract appointment, renewable annually up to a total of 3 years. All other selected candidates (Posts 1-29) will be governed by the National Pension System (NPS) unless already covered under CCS (Pension) Rules 1972. | Candidates selected for posts at Sr. Nos. 12 to 17 and 22 to 29 will be required to work on Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays and must be prepared for shift duties as per institutional requirements.

selection_process: Post Mode of Recruitment
Scientific Officer (C) - Post 1 Personal Interview
Scientific Officer (C) - Posts 2 & 3 Written Test and Personal Interview
Junior Engineer (B) Personal Interview
Scientific Assistant (B) - Post 5 Personal Interview
Scientific Assistant (B) - Post 6 Written Examination and Personal Interview
Scientific Assistant (B) - Post 7 Written Test and Personal Interview or Personal Interview only
Administrative Assistant (B) - Post 8 Written Test and Skill Test
Administrative Assistant (B) - Post 9 Written Test and Skill Test
Administrative Assistant (B) - Post 10 Written Test and Skill Test
Supervisor (Cosmetics Maintenance) Written Test and Skill Test
Tradesman (B) [Plumber, Electrician, Welder, Mechatronics] Written Test and Trade Test
Laboratory Assistant (B) Written Test and Trade Test
Clerk (A) - all posts Written Test and Skill Test
Work Assistant (Auxiliary) - all posts Written Test and Skill Test
Work Assistant (Technical) - all posts Written Test and Trade Test
Security Guard Written Test, Physical Fitness Test, and Physical Efficiency Test
Project Administrative Assistant (B) Written Test and Skill Test

The selection mode for TIFR 2026 varies by post category. Candidates should note that merely fulfilling the minimum qualifications does not guarantee a call for the recruitment process — TIFR reserves the right to shortlist candidates based on qualifications and experience higher than the prescribed minimum.
TIFR reserves the right to conduct all written tests
skill tests
and interviews either online or in-person.
how_to_apply: Applications for TIFR Recruitment 2026 must be submitted online only at tifr.res.in. No offline or postal applications will be accepted. Government/Semi-Government/PSU employees, if called for the selection process, must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer.
Visit the official TIFR website at tifr.res.in.
Navigate to the Careers / Recruitment section.
Click on and read the full notification carefully.
Click "Apply Online" for the relevant post.
Register and fill in all personal, educational, and experience details accurately.
Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and relevant certificates.
Submit the online application before 4 July 2026.
Take a printout of the completed application form for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria - Qualification & Age Limit
content_sections.text: Each post under TIFR Recruitment 2026 carries specific eligibility requirements. The age limit is reckoned as on the closing date for receipt of applications (4 July 2026) . Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India rules for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen/Government servant candidates. content_sections.heading: TIFR Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection mode for TIFR 2026 varies by post category. Candidates should note that merely fulfilling the minimum qualifications does not guarantee a call for the recruitment process — TIFR reserves the right to shortlist candidates based on qualifications and experience higher than the prescribed minimum. TIFR reserves the right to conduct all written tests, skill tests, and interviews either online or in-person.

rows.mode_of_recruitment: Written Test and Personal Interview or Personal Interview only
Written Test, Physical Fitness Test, and Physical Efficiency Test

content_sections.heading: TIFR Recruitment 2026 - How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applications for TIFR Recruitment 2026 must be submitted online only at tifr.res.in. No offline or postal applications will be accepted. Government/Semi-Government/PSU employees, if called for the selection process, must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer. Visit the official TIFR website at tifr.res.in . Navigate to the Careers / Recruitment section. Click on and read the full notification carefully. Click "Apply Online" for the relevant post. Register and fill in all personal, educational, and experience details accurately. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and relevant certificates. Submit the online application before 4 July 2026 . Take a printout of the completed application form for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for TIFR Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates called for the selection process must bring originals along with copies of the following documents: Printout of the online application form Valid photo identity proof (Aadhaar Card / Voter ID / PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence) Date of birth proof Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (all semesters/years) Experience certificates Character certificates from two respectable persons SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD certificate in Government of India format (wherever applicable) OBC Non-Creamy Layer certificate valid preferably for the current financial year EWS certificate valid preferably for the current financial year PwBD candidates must submit Disability Certificate showing minimum 40% disability

content_sections.heading: Salary & Appointment Terms
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about appointment duration can note that for posts at Sr. Nos. 1 to 7 and 12 to 17, the initial appointment will be for 3 years including a 1-year probation period . Continuation beyond 3 years is subject to comprehensive performance review; upon successful review, the appointment continues till superannuation at age 60. Post No. 30 (Project Administrative Assistant) is a temporary, non-pensionable contract appointment, renewable annually up to a total of 3 years. All other selected candidates (Posts 1-29) will be governed by the National Pension System (NPS) unless already covered under CCS (Pension) Rules 1972. Candidates selected for posts at Sr. Nos. 12 to 17 and 22 to 29 will be required to work on Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays and must be prepared for shift duties as per institutional requirements.

content_sections.heading: Who Should NOT Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates who have completed only a part-time or correspondence degree where the notification requires a full-time degree. OBC candidates without a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate for the current financial year. Candidates who cannot produce grade-to-percentage conversion certificates from their university, where applicable. Candidates already in government service who are unable to obtain an NOC from their employer before the selection stage. Candidates for Security Guard posts who do not meet the prescribed physical standards (height, chest, eyesight).

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for TIFR Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for TIFR Advertisement No. 2026/6 is 4 July 2026. Applications after this date will not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q2. How many total posts are advertised under TIFR Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: TIFR has advertised 35 posts across scientific, technical, administrative, and support categories under Advertisement No. 2026/6, all tenable at TIFR Colaba, Mumbai.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for Scientific Officer (C) at TIFR?
faq.answer: The Total Monthly Emoluments (TME) for Scientific Officer (C) at TIFR is ₹1,18,110 at Pay Level-10, Stage-1 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for TIFR Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The notification does not specify a separate application fee structure for this advertisement. Applications are accepted online only at tifr.res.in.

faq.question: Q5. What is the mode of selection for TIFR Technical and Tradesman posts?
faq.answer: Tradesman (B) posts are filled through a Written Test followed by a Trade Test. Scientific Officer posts are selected via Written Test and Personal Interview or Personal Interview only, depending on the specific post.

faq.question: Q6. Can SC/ST/OBC candidates apply for Unreserved (UR) posts in TIFR Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates from SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and PwBD categories may apply for UR posts. However, age relaxation is not available to SC/ST/OBC candidates applying against unreserved vacancies.

faq.question: Q7. Is TIFR Recruitment 2026 a permanent appointment?
faq.answer: For most posts (Sr. Nos. 1–7 and 12–17), the initial appointment is for 3 years including 1 year probation, continuing till superannuation at 60 after a performance review. Post No. 30 (Project Administrative Assistant) is a temporary, non-pensionable contract for up to 3 years.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for Clerk (A) posts in TIFR 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age for Clerk (A) UR posts is 28 years. For the OBC post (Sr. No. 18), the maximum age is 31 years. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for TIFR Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for TIFR Advertisement No. 2026/6 is 4 July 2026. Applications after this date will not be accepted under any circumstances.
